plan: 1. **Add skip-to-main navigation link** — In `src/layout/shell.ts`, insert a visually-hidden skip link (`Skip to main content`) as the first child of the shell container. Add `.skip-link` styles in `src/styles/global.css` (visually hidden until focused, then positioned at top of viewport with high z-index).

2. **Add missing ARIA labels to buttons** — In `src/ui/rerun-button.ts`, add `aria-label='Re-run audit'`. Review all button components (`copy-button.ts`, `share-image-button.ts`, `rerun-button.ts`) and ensure `aria-busy` and `aria-disabled` are toggled during async operations (e.g., copy-to-clipboard, image export).

3. **Ensure visible focus states on all interactive elements** — In `src/styles/global.css` or `src/styles/button.css`, add a global `:focus-visible` rule for all focusable elements (`a, button, [tabindex]`) with `outline: 2px solid var(--color-accent); outline-offset: 2px`. Verify this doesn't conflict with the existing `.btn:focus-visible` rule.

4. **Verify and fix color contrast** — Audit all color token combinations in `src/styles/tokens.css` against WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). Key pairs to check: `--color-text-muted` (#6b7280) on `--color-bg` (#ffffff), `--color-accent` (#4a7c6f) on white, severity badge colors on their backgrounds. Adjust any failing values while maintaining the design direction.

5. **Add ARIA landmarks and labels to sections** — Add `aria-label` attributes to each `<section>` element in module renderers (`zero-click-osint-module.ts`, `shadow-profile-module.ts`, `threat-model-module.ts`, `hardening-module.ts`, `permission-debt` module) so screen readers can list landmarks meaningfully (e.g., `aria-label='Zero-Click OSINT'`).

6. **Improve intro overlay accessibility** — In `src/modules/intro/intro-sequence.ts`, add `role='status'` and `aria-live='polite'` to the intro overlay so screen readers announce the intro content. Ensure the overlay is removed from the DOM (not just hidden) when complete, or add `aria-hidden='true'` when dismissed.

7. **Ensure keyboard navigation flow is logical** — Verify tab order follows visual layout (header → skip link target → receipt actions → module sections → footer). Ensure no focusable elements are hidden behind the intro overlay. If the intro overlay is visible, either trap focus or ensure underlying content is inert (`inert` attribute on shell while overlay is active).

8. **Write accessibility tests** — Create `tests/us-032/accessibility.test.ts` with Vitest tests covering: (a) skip link exists and points to #dashboard, (b) all buttons have aria-labels, (c) all sections have aria-labels, (d) focus-visible styles are defined in CSS, (e) semantic landmark structure is correct (header > h1, main, footer, sections), (f) color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA for all token pairs.
9. **Run lint, typecheck, and test suite** — Execute `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify no regressions. Fix any issues found.

10. **Manual keyboard walkthrough** — Tab through the entire page in a browser to verify: every interactive element receives focus, focus indicator is visible, tab order is logical, no focus traps (except intentional ones), Escape dismisses any overlays, and all content is reachable via keyboard alone.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` after all changes
Create `tests/us-032/accessibility.test.ts` covering: skip link, aria-labels on buttons, aria-labels on sections, semantic landmarks, focus-visible CSS rules, color contrast ratios
Manual keyboard-only navigation test: tab through entire page verifying focus visibility and logical order
Test with browser accessibility audit tools (Chrome DevTools Lighthouse accessibility audit, axe DevTools extension)
Verify reduced-motion existing tests still pass (tests/us-033/reduced-motion.test.ts)
Test in multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) for focus-visible support differences
risks: Color contrast adjustments to tokens may subtly shift the visual design — verify with design direction (Stripe + threat intelligence lab aesthetic)
Adding `inert` attribute to shell during intro overlay may not be supported in all target browsers — check compatibility or use a polyfill
Global focus-visible styles could conflict with existing button-specific focus styles — test specificity carefully
Intro overlay accessibility changes must not break existing reduced-motion tests in tests/us-033/
aria-label on sections duplicates the visible h2 text — consider using aria-labelledby referencing the h2 id instead to avoid content duplication
